informal: Prove that if $p > q$ are two primes such that $q \mid p - 1$, then any two nonabelian groups of order $pq$ are isomorphic.
lean:
theorem Herstein_exercise_2_8_15 {G H: Type*} [Fintype G] [Group G] [Fintype H] [Group H]
  (hG_nonab : ∃ x y : G, x * y ≠ y * x) (hH_nonab : ∃ x y : H, x * y ≠ y * x)
  {p q : ℕ} (hp : Nat.Prime p) (hq : Nat.Prime q)
  (h : p > q) (h1 : q ∣ p - 1) (hG : card G = p*q) (hH : card H = p*q) :
  Nonempty (G ≃* H)
reasoning: The Lean signature captures the informal claim exactly: G and H are finite groups (Fintype + Group), both nonabelian (encoded as existence of non-commuting elements, which is the standard negation of commutativity), p and q are primes with p > q and q ∣ p − 1, and both groups have order pq. The conclusion Nonempty (G ≃* H) gives a multiplicative isomorphism, matching "isomorphic." The quantifier structure ("any two nonabelian groups") is correctly rendered by taking arbitrary G and H with these properties and concluding they are isomorphic. No extra hypotheses are added and none are dropped.
